summary: P1 test-lane registry authority now blocks persistent marker/Makefile/CI matrix drift, and follow-up validation confirmed the updated registry/Makefile/docs contract through targeted, broader touched-surface, and full-suite runs. The closeout path now refreshes script-output-surfaces before finalization, and artifact-finalization explicitly checks script-output-surfaces currentness so adjacent generated-artifact writer drift is detected before the final full-suite pass.

observations.surface: ops/test-lane-registry.json, Makefile report-contract targets, tests/test_ci_workflow_static.py, tests/test_release_workflow_static.py
observations.problem: The lane registry now blocks marker, Makefile lane-expression, and CI matrix drift for persistent lanes, but report-contract execution still depends on hand-curated selector packs and workflow static tests remain only partially semantic. Registry authority does not yet drive all derived packs or all workflow assertions end-to-end.
observations.why_it_matters: Partial authority leaves a second drift path: lane taxonomy can stay consistent while curated selectors or substring-based workflow checks quietly diverge from the intended execution contract.
observations.automation_candidate: Extend the registry schema from persistent lane metadata into derived pack and workflow-step contracts, then validate or generate report-contract selectors and workflow lane assertions from the same registry-backed source.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep registry-derived semantic validation for lane taxonomy and execution wiring, and continue maintaining post-change regression bundles so adjacent generated-artifact writers remain covered whenever new generated registries or closeout artifacts are introduced.
